expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` before any build/benchmark work.
expectations.evidence: '`winml inspect google/vit-base-patch16-224` â€” non-negotiable first step.'

expectations.text: Identifies QNN as the EP for Snapdragon X Elite NPU.
expectations.evidence: 'Generate a config targeting QNN and run `winml build`' + 'QNN-compiled artifact'.

expectations.text: Recommends building once and running `winml perf` twice.
expectations.evidence: Section header literally 'Build once, perf twice.' with rationale: 'you want both runs to share the same export... otherwise you're measuring pipeline differences, not hardware differences.'

expectations.text: Specifies that CPU run uses optimized (pre-compile) artifact, NOT QNN-compiled.
expectations.evidence: 'the optimized ONNX (pre-compile, EP-agnostic) â€” use this for the CPU run' + 'the QNN-compiled artifact â€” use this for the NPU run'.
'Don't run the QNN-compiled model on CPU EP (or vice versa). Compiled artifacts are EP-locked â€” you'll get nonsense numbers or a load failure.'

expectations.text: Directs to --help or uses common patterns; no fabricated flags.
expectations.evidence: 'Run `winml --help`, `winml perf --help`, and `winml build --help` to confirm the current flag names... I'd rather you read the live flags than have me guess names that may have drifted.'

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable — every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: verify_commands.py found 1 broken command(s): - `winml inspect google/vit-base-patch16-224` has positional arg(s) ['google/vit-base-patch16-224'] but `winml inspect` is flag-only
